skill.name: wireshark-analysis
skill.description: Execute comprehensive network traffic analysis using Wireshark to capture, filter, and examine network packets for security investigations, performance optimization, and troubleshooting.
skill.summary: AI-powered assistant for Wireshark network traffic analysis, packet capture, and security investigation workflows.
skill.author: zebbern

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains Wireshark command documentation in markdown code blocks, not executable code. No actual code execution, network operations, or cryptographic implementations are present. This is educational documentation for network security professionals.

content.value_statement: Network security professionals spend hours manually filtering packet captures and reconstructing communication streams. This skill provides instant Wireshark expertise with proper filter syntax, stream analysis workflows, and security detection patterns.
actual_capabilities: Generate Wireshark display filters for IP addresses, protocols, ports, and TCP flags
Guide step-by-step through packet capture, filtering, and stream reconstruction workflows
Explain expert information findings like TCP retransmissions, duplicate ACKs, and zero window events
Provide security analysis patterns for detecting port scans, ARP spoofing, and malware C2 traffic
Export analysis results with proper documentation for incident reports
limitations: Cannot directly execute Wireshark or access network interfaces - requires manual tool operation
Does not analyze PCAP files automatically - users must provide packet details for interpretation
Cannot decrypt encrypted traffic without proper TLS key configuration
Large capture files require manual filtering and navigation by the user

use_cases.title: Incident Response Investigation
use_cases.description: Security analysts investigating suspected malware infections or data exfiltration use filter syntax to identify suspicious traffic patterns, extract C2 beaconing behavior, and document evidence.

use_cases.title: Network Performance Troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Network engineers diagnosing slow application performance use TCP analysis filters to identify retransmissions, packet loss, and zero window conditions affecting throughput.

use_cases.title: Security Education and Training
use_cases.description: Students learning network protocols use guided workflows to understand TCP handshakes, follow HTTP streams, and practice packet-level analysis techniques.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Filter Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Wireshark display filter to show all HTTP traffic from IP address 192.168.1.100.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to isolate web traffic from a specific host for investigation.

prompt_templates.title: TCP Stream Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through following a TCP stream in Wireshark to reconstruct a complete conversation between two hosts. I need to see the actual data exchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: Investigator needs to view the full content of a network conversation.

prompt_templates.title: Security Pattern Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create Wireshark filters to detect potential port scanning activity. I want to identify hosts that are attempting connections to multiple ports.
prompt_templates.scenario: Security analyst investigating reconnaissance activity on the network.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshooting Network Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm experiencing slow network performance. Show me how to use Wireshark expert information to identify TCP retransmissions, duplicate ACKs, and other indicators of network problems.
prompt_templates.scenario: Network engineer diagnosing performance degradation issues.

output_examples.input: Create a filter to show all DNS traffic to external servers only.
output_examples.output: Use this display filter: dns && !(ip.addr == 192.168.1.0/24). This shows all DNS queries and responses except those within your local network. Adjust the network range to match your internal subnet.

output_examples.input: How do I find plaintext credentials in HTTP traffic?
output_examples.output: Follow these steps: 1) Filter with http.request.method == "POST" to find form submissions. 2) Right-click any POST packet and select Follow > HTTP Stream. 3) Search the stream for 'username', 'password', 'email', or 'login' parameters. 4) Look in the request body for credentials transmitted in cleartext.

best_practices: Always use capture filters before starting live captures to limit data collection and reduce memory usage on high-traffic networks.
Save PCAP files with descriptive names and timestamps before performing destructive filtering operations to preserve original evidence.
Document your analysis methodology and findings in notes for incident reports and future reference.
anti_patterns: Avoid deleting packets from the capture view permanently - use display filters instead to hide packets without losing data.
Do not capture sensitive credentials or personal data unnecessarily - use targeted capture filters to minimize privacy impact.
Never share PCAP files containing sensitive network data without redacting or encrypting them first.

faq.question: Can this skill directly analyze my PCAP files?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides expertise and guidance for Wireshark workflows. You must run Wireshark separately and describe what you find for interpretation and analysis assistance.

faq.question: Why does my filter show red in Wireshark?
faq.answer: A red filter indicates syntax errors. Check for typos in field names, missing operators, or unbalanced parentheses. Use the Expression button to browse valid filter fields.

faq.question: Can I decrypt HTTPS traffic with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill explains the decryption process, but you need the server's private key or browser pre-master secret key. Modern TLS with forward secrecy often prevents passive decryption.

faq.question: What is the difference between capture and display filters?
faq.answer: Capture filters limit what Wireshark records before saving packets, reducing file size. Display filters hide packets in the view after capture, letting you show or hide traffic without losing data.

faq.question: How do I extract files from HTTP traffic?
faq.answer: Go to File > Export Objects > HTTP. Wireshark lists all files transferred via HTTP with content types. Select files to export and save them to disk for analysis.

faq.question: Can this skill detect malware in network traffic?
faq.answer: This skill provides patterns and techniques for identifying suspicious traffic like C2 beaconing, unusual ports, and encoded payloads. Detection requires manual analysis and threat intelligence context.
